items.title: Remediate Hardcoded Secrets in Source Code

details.what: Multiple high-entropy secrets and API keys are hardcoded in the source code.
details.why: Hardcoded secrets can be easily extracted by attackers, leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, and severe security incidents.

details.what: Seven distinct hardcoded secrets (Stripe, AWS, GitHub, Slack, JWT, RSA private key, generic API key) are present in src/config.js.
details.why: Committing sensitive credentials into version control exposes them to anyone with repository access and increases the risk of credential leakage.

items.title: Extract 7 secrets from src/config.js into environment variables

details.what: Migrate the 7 hardcoded secrets from src/config.js to environment variables.
details.why: To securely manage credentials, they must be injected via the environment at runtime rather than stored statically in the codebase.
details.how: Replace the string literals in src/config.js with process.env lookups and update the deployment configuration to inject these secrets.
details.constraints: The application must remain functional in local development and production without downtime during the migration.

5m.man: Developer time to refactor code and update documentation.
5m.machine: Secrets management system or CI/CD variable configuration.
5m.material: Source code repository and environment configuration files.
5m.measurement: Automated secret scanning confirms zero exposed secrets.
